text: Surface recurring system friction from supervisor-errors.log into the next session's context
description: SessionMemory.summarizeRecentFriction aggregates the durable ERROR-state log into a ranked "Recent System Friction" block (busy-timeout, quota, worker-stuck, parse failure, …) and loadSessionMemoryContext appends it to the instruct context. Gives each run real, structured limitation data about its own recurring failures. Tested (bucketing, aggregation, empty, malformed-skip).

reflections.context: First concrete slice of b03ad58e (self-reflection / system-limitation analysis). Discovered the existing session reflection is a stub: extractReflection builds a prompt but never sends it, so patterns/lessons are always empty and the injected 'Previous Session Insights' are noisy regex scrapes of terminal scrollback.
reflections.outcome: Rather than fix the fragile LLM-reflection path overnight, surfaced the already-structured ERROR log (supervisor-errors.log) as a ranked friction summary injected into the next session's context. Shared the log-dir resolver between writer and reader so paths can't drift.
reflections.effectiveness: High value-to-risk: pure-function aggregation, fully unit-tested (4 tests), no change to the live control flow beyond appending a context block. Real limitation data instead of heuristic scrollback.
reflections.lesson: When a feature's 'proper' implementation is LLM-dependent and hard to verify unattended, look for structured data the system already logs. The durable ERROR log was a higher-signal source than the reflection stub it sits next to.
reflections.pattern: Prefer feeding back data the system already records over inventing new fragile extraction.

status.comment: Shipped in commit 1323534 on branch feat/autonomous-improvements. 4 new tests green.
